So the trouble with sleep, the changes in eating, and the general feeling of heaviness all started around February after the breakup. That gives me a clear picture of what's been going on for you recently.

To understand more about your story, I'd like to ask a bit about your family. Could you tell me your mother's name and age, or if she's deceased, when she passed away and what caused it?

turns.text: Patricia is 58, and she’s a nurse, so she’

turns.text: Okay, Patricia, 58, a nurse. And what about your father? What's his name and age, or if he's deceased, when did he pass away and what was the cause?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Robert is 60.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Thanks. So, your mother Patricia is 58, and your father Robert is 60. Are your parents still together?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: They’ve been married

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
Okay, so Patricia and Robert are still married.

Do you have any brothers or sisters?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Amanda is my older sister, she’s 32.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Okay, Amanda, 32. Do you have any other brothers or sisters?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Relation to Amanda:* Amanda and I are close-ish, I guess?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
Thanks for sharing that you and Amanda are close-ish.

Do you have any other brothers or sisters besides Amanda?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: No, just the

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
Okay, so just you and Amanda.

Now, I'd like to ask about your grandparents. Starting with your mother's side, could you tell me your maternal grandmother's name and whether she's alive or deceased? If she's deceased, when did she pass away and what was the cause?

turns.text: Helen was my grandma

turns.speaker: ai

turns.text: She passed away in

turns.speaker: ai

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: June 2022 was when she finally passed, though

turns.text: Thanks for clarifying, so Helen passed away in June 2022. Do you know what caused her death?

Okay, so Helen passed away in June 2022 from Alzheimer's.

What about your maternal grandfather? What's his name, and is he alive or deceased? If he's deceased, when did he pass away and what was the cause?

turns.text: Okay, so your maternal grandfather, Frank, passed away in October 2015. Do you know what caused his death?

So the trouble with sleep, the changes in eating, and the general feeling of heaviness all started around February after the breakup. That gives me a clear picture of what's been going on for you recently.

To understand more about your story, I'd like to ask a bit about your family. Could you tell me your mother's name and age, or if she's deceased, when she passed away and what caused it?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: My oldest is 14 and starting to pull away. I know it's developmentally normal but it's getting to me more than I expected.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Thanks for sharing that. It's definitely a common experience. Can you tell me a bit more about what "pulling away" looks like with your oldest? And when did you first start noticing these changes?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: It started about a year ago, right

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Okay, so about a year ago. Can you give me some examples of what that pulling away looks like? What are you noticing specifically?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Ethan is just... different.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What kinds of differences are you seeing? Can you give me an example of how he's different now compared to a year ago?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: The door is the biggest thing.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The door. What happens with the door?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: It’s just closed.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: So, the door is closed. Has that always been the case, or is this a new development? And how does that affect things in the house?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: It used to be wide open.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Okay, so it used to be wide open, and now it's closed. How does that feel for you when you see his door closed?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: It feels like a wall I didn't

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: It sounds like a significant change for you, feeling that wall. Beyond the closed door, what other ways is Ethan pulling away? Are there other things you've noticed that are different in his behavior or your interactions?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: He doesn't look up from his phone when I walk in anymore.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Okay, so he's not looking up from his phone when you walk in. How does that affect your conversations with him? And how does your partner or other children in the house experience these changes with Ethan?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Michael says I’

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Michael says what? Can you tell me what he's noticed or what his perspective is on Ethan pulling away?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Michael thinks I’m overreacting.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: So Michael thinks you're overreacting. What does he say when you talk to him about it? And do you have other children besides Ethan?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Olivia is 11 and Ben is

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
Okay, so Olivia is 11 and Ben is 8.

Circling back to Michael for a moment, what does he say specifically when he tells you you're overreacting? And how do Olivia and Ben react to Ethan's door being closed or him being on his phone?

turns.text: Michael just says

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Michael just says what? What are his exact words or his main point when he says you're overreacting? And how do Olivia and Ben seem to be handling Ethan's changes?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
Okay, so Michael says, "He's fourteen, Jen." It sounds like he sees it as a normal part of adolescence.

How do Olivia and Ben react to Ethan's door being closed or him being on his phone? Do they say anything about it, or do you notice any changes in their interactions with Ethan?

turns.text: Ben and Olivia don't really seem to notice the same way I do,

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text:
It sounds like you're feeling the changes with Ethan much more acutely than the rest of the family. That gives me a good picture of what's happening now.

To get a broader sense of your family, I'd like to ask a bit about your parents. Can you tell me your mother's name and age, or if she's passed away, when that was?

turns.speaker: ai

That's a long time.
